info.title: ExamPro — Secure Online Examination API
info.description:
Public API surface of the microservices platform, exposed through the **API gateway**.

### How routing works
- `auth-service` runs **in-process** in the gateway → reachable at `/api/auth/*`.
- Domain services are **reverse-proxied** at `/api/modules/<service>/*`.
  The gateway strips the `/api/modules/<service>` prefix and forwards to the
  service's own base path:

| Public prefix | Forwarded to (internal) |
|---|---|
| `/api/modules/user` | user-service `/api/users` |
| `/api/modules/exam` | exam-service `/api/exam` |
| `/api/modules/student-exam` | student-exam `/api/v1/student-exam` |
| `/api/modules/grade-cheat` | grade-cheat `/api` |
| `/api/modules/log` | log-service `/logs` |
| `/api/modules/risk` | risk-service `/risk` |

### Authentication
Most routes need a JWT access token: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Obtain it
from `POST /api/auth/login`. The gateway verifies the token **once**, strips any
client-supplied `x-user-*` headers, and forwards a trusted identity plus an
`x-service-secret` to the service. Click **Authorize** and paste your access token
to use *Try it out*. The refresh token is an httpOnly cookie set by the server.

### Response envelopes (not yet unified)
Different services wrap responses differently — `{ success, data }`,
`{ status, data, message }`, or a gateway error `{ status, error_code, message, timestamp }`.
The frontend `unwrap()` tolerates all shapes.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Registration, login, sessions, password reset (in-process auth-service).

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Profiles, instructor approval, student lookup (user-service / PostgreSQL).

data.description: Access token (JWT). Refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie.

referenceAnswer.description: Correct answer. **Stripped** from responses sent to students.

studentId.description: Taken from the JWT, never the body.

deviceFingerprint.description: SHA-256 hash, not raw device data.

200.description: OTP emailed; temp data stored in Redis.

post.summary: Confirm OTP → create auth record + profile
Login → access token (+ refresh cookie, single active session)

409.description: Already logged in on another device.

post.summary: Mint a new access token from the refresh cookie
Revoke all sessions for the user
Email a reset OTP (no account enumeration)

200.description: Generic success message regardless of existence.

post.summary: Set a new password (requires verified reset OTP)
Add a question to an exam (owner teacher)
Submit an attempt (studentId from JWT; one submission per student)

get.summary: A student's own graded result (score + feedback, no integrity internals)
get.description: Returns the calling student's submission joined with grading output. Plagiarism / cheating signals are stripped — students see scores and feedback only. 404 if they never submitted.

200.description: Own result (status, answers, per-question scores/feedback, own violations).

get.description: Students read **their own**; teachers/admins may pass `?studentId=`.

post.summary: Enqueue grading for an exam (returns taskId)

delete.summary: Delete a grading task + release its lock

get.summary: Stored grading results for an exam

200.description: Chain status (valid or first break index).
{ nodes, links, counts } for the in-app force-directed graph.
